segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: OK Rosie, shall we try to get some ideas together for our presentation on diet and obesity?
segments.zh: 好的，罗茜，我们是不是该为关于饮食和肥胖的演讲整理一下思路？

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Sure.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: I can talk about the experiment I did to see if people can tell the difference between real sugar and artificial sweeteners.
segments.zh: 我可以讲讲我做的那个实验，看人们能不能区分真糖和人工甜味剂。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Where you gave people drinks with either sugar or artificial sweeteners and they had to say which they thought it was?
segments.zh: 就是你给人们喝加糖或人工甜味剂的饮料，然后让他们猜是哪种？

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 对。我花了好长时间才决定好具体怎么组织，尤其是怎么确保人们不知道我给他们喝的是哪种饮料。很难把所有事情都记清楚，特别是参与的人很多——我得确保准确记录每个人喝的是什么。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: It took me ages to decide exactly how I'd organise it, especially how I could make sure that people didn't know which drink I was giving them.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: It was hard to keep track of it all, especially as I had so many people doing it – I had to make sure I kept a proper record of what each person had had.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: So could most people tell the difference?
segments.zh: 那大多数人能分辨出来吗？

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Yeah – I hadn't thought they would be able to, but most people could.
segments.zh: 能——我本来以为他们分不出来，但大多数人可以。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Then there's that experiment I did measuring the fat content of nuts, to see if the nutritional information given on the packet was accurate.
segments.zh: 还有我做的那个测量坚果脂肪含量的实验，看看包装上的营养信息是否准确。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: The one where you ground up the nuts and mixed them with a chemical to absorb the fat?
segments.zh: 就是你把坚果磨碎，然后和一种化学物质混合来吸收脂肪的那个实验？

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。我的结果有点问题——那种坚果的脂肪含量似乎比包装上标明的低很多。但我认为包装上的信息是对的。我觉得我可能应该把坚果磨得更碎一些。另外，称脂肪的天平可能也不够精确。我真想什么时候再重做一次这个实验。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: My results were a bit problematic – the fat content for that type of nut seemed much lower than it said on the package.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: But I reckon the package information was right.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I think I should probably have ground up the nuts more than I did.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: It's possible that the scales for weighing the fat weren't accurate enough, too.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I'd really like to try the experiment again some time.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: So what can we say about helping people to lose weight?
segments.zh: 那么关于帮助人们减肥我们能说些什么呢？关于餐馆可以采取哪些措施来减少肥胖，我们有很多可以说的。我读到过，菜单开头和结尾的菜品比中间的更容易被选中。所以，如果你把低热量的菜品放在菜单的开头和结尾，人们很可能会选择热量更少的食物，甚至自己都没意识到。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: There's a lot we could say about what restaurants could do to reduce obesity.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: I read that the items at the start of a menu and the items at the end of a menu are much more likely to be chosen than the items in the middle.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: So, if you put the low-calorie items at the beginning and end of the menu, people will probably go for the food with fewer calories, without even realising what they're doing.

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: I think food manufacturers could do more to encourage healthy eating.
segments.zh: 我认为食品制造商可以做得更多来鼓励健康饮食。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: How?

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Well, when manufacturers put calorie counts of a food on the label, they're sometimes really confusing and I suspect they do it on purpose.
segments.zh: 嗯，当制造商在食品标签上标注卡路里含量时，有时真的很令人困惑，我怀疑他们是故意的。因为高热量食物味道更好，这样他们就能卖得更多。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Because food that's high in calories tastes better, and so they'll sell more.

items.prompt: Which TWO points does Adam make about his experiment on artificial sweeteners?

pairs.q: make sure that people didn't know which drink I was giving them
pairs.p: make sure that people didn't know which drink I was giving them
pairs.note: 题干未直接出现，但原文描述了实验设计的关键点，对应选项C（控制变量）。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Adam关于人工甜味剂实验的两个观点，原文中Adam提到他花时间决定如何组织实验，特别是如何确保受试者不知道喝的是哪种饮料，这对应选项C（控制变量）。

items.prompt: Which TWO points does Adam make about his experiment on artificial sweeteners?

pairs.q: make sure that people didn't know which drink
pairs.p: make sure that people didn't know which drink
pairs.note: 题干中关于实验设计的要点，原文直接出现，帮助定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Adam关于人工甜味剂实验的两点，原文中直接提到实验设计和确保参与者不知道饮料种类，对应选项D（确保参与者不知道他们喝的是什么）。

items.prompt: Which TWO problems did Rosie have when measuring the fat content of nuts?

pairs.note: problematic 是 problems 的同根词，表示有问题。
原文直接出现 fat content，与题干测量脂肪含量对应。
weren't accurate enough 是 problems 的具体表现，表示不准确的问题。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 Rosie 测量坚果脂肪含量时遇到的两个问题，定位句提到结果有问题（problematic），并具体说明脂肪含量偏低、可能研磨不够、称重不准确等，其中称重不准确（weren't accurate enough）是问题之一。

items.prompt: Which TWO problems did Rosie have when measuring the fat content of nuts?

pairs.note: problematic 是 problems 的同义词，表示有问题。
原文直接出现 fat content，与题干测量脂肪含量对应。
weren't accurate enough 是 problems 的具体表现，表示不准确的问题。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 Rosie 在测量坚果脂肪含量时遇到的两个问题，定位句提到结果有问题（problematic），并具体指出脂肪含量偏低、研磨不够、称重不准确等，其中脂肪含量偏低和称重不准确对应选项 E。

items.prompt: Adam suggests that restaurants could reduce obesity if their menus

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。
题干复数menus对应原文单数menu，但词根相同，直接匹配。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中'reduce obesity'和'menus'在原文中直接出现，可快速定位到餐厅措施部分。

items.prompt: The students agree that food manufacturers deliberately

pairs.note: deliberately 和 on purpose 都表示'故意地'，是同义替换。
原文中的 manufacturers 直接对应题干中的 food manufacturers，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问学生同意食品制造商故意做什么，定位句提到制造商故意把卡路里标签弄得很 confusing，其中 on purpose 对应 deliberately，因此选 A。

items.prompt: What does Rosie say about levels of exercise in England?
Adam refers to the location and width of stairs in a train station to illustrate
What do the students agree about including reference to exercise in their presentation?
What are the students going to do next for their presentation?
